sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Udinese, currently sitting at 12th in the league with 44 points, face a tougher challenge as they host Bologna, who are in 7th place with an impressive 62 points. This Round 34 clash at the Bluenergy Stadium in Udine is set to be an intriguing encounter. With Bologna aiming to solidify their higher league standing, Udinese will look to capitalize on home advantage to bridge the gap.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form and Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Udinese are struggling with a streak of no wins in their last 6 matches and have failed to score in their last 3 home games. Bologna, on the other hand, have shown a knack for scoring first in 6 of their last 8 away matches. Historically, in the last 5 matchups between these sides, Bologna have secured 2 wins, Udinese have 1, and 2 games ended in draws. Their most recent meeting on August 18, 2024, resulted in a 1-1 draw, with Udinese's Lautaro Giannetti earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.4 Sofascore rating.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Udinese, Oumar Solet stands out with a league rank of 5 for his 7.33 average rating and 9.1% long balls in total passes, also ranking 5th in the league. Martín Payero has been a consistent performer against Bologna, averaging a 7.5 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matchups with 2 goals scored. Bologna’s attack is led by Riccardo Orsolini, who ranks 5th in the league with 12 goals scored this season. Additionally, Juan Miranda has been pivotal with 55 key passes (5th in the league) and 10 big chances created (4th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Team Strengths and Weaknesses
sections.text: Udinese have a strong aerial presence, ranking 5th in the league for aerial duels won at 51.83%, and lead the league in interceptions per game with 8.14. However, their offensive output is underwhelming, with only 38 goals scored this season (13th in the league) and a concerning streak of conceding in their last 6 home games. Bologna excel in late-game scoring, with 26.32% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their defense, however, has struggled to keep clean sheets in recent head-to-heads against Udinese.

sections.subtitle: Notable Absences
sections.text: Udinese will miss key forwards Lorenzo Lucca, who has 10 goals this season, and Florian Thauvin with 8 goals and 3 assists, which could severely impact their attacking threat. Bologna are without Dan Ndoye, a midfielder who leads the league in fouls drawn per game at 3.3 and has contributed 8 goals and 3 assists. These absences could shift the tactical balance of the match.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Udinese’s strength lies in defensive solidity, ranking 1st in interceptions per game at 8.14 and showing proficiency in headers with 9 scored (4th in the league). Bologna will likely exploit their creative edge, with players like Juan Miranda providing width and key passes. Udinese’s timing of goals shows a peak between the 31st and 45th minutes with 22.45% of their goals, while Bologna thrive in the closing stages, which could lead to a tense finish.

sections.subtitle: Referee and Disciplinary Expectations
sections.text: Referee Fabio Maresca, who averages 5.6 yellow cards and 0.42 red cards per game, will oversee this match. Given Udinese’s league-high foul count of 494 (3rd most) and 78 yellow cards (16th least), paired with historical trends of less than 4.5 cards in 8 of Udinese’s last 10 home games, we can expect a moderately disciplined encounter. Bologna’s recent games have also shown a trend of higher card counts, which could add to the tally.

sections.subtitle: Stadium and Crowd Factor
sections.text: The Bluenergy Stadium in Udine, home to Udinese, will be a crucial factor as the hosts look to break their no-win streak of 6 games. With a home crowd behind them, players like Sandi Lovrić (2 goals, 3 assists) and Hassane Kamara (1 goal, 4 assists) could be inspired to push for a much-needed result. Bologna, however, have shown resilience on the road and will aim to quiet the home support.
